title: Vedana Feeling
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and systematic treatment of vedana (feeling) as the critical turning point in dependent origination. It masterfully weaves together the technical Buddhist psychology with practical meditation instruction, personal anecdotes from Bhante's journey through Zen to Theravada, and clear explanations of how feeling sits between conditioned experience and our reactive craving. The structure moves logically from the four elements through to the Satipatthana instructions, making it an ideal spine for a chapter on this theme.
summary: This discourse systematically explains vedana (feeling) as the pivotal link in dependent origination where meditators can interrupt the cycle of suffering. Bhante draws on personal experience and canonical sources to show how staying present with pleasant/unpleasant feelings—without indulging or suppressing—liberates consciousness from its attachments while allowing psychological healing to occur naturally.

relevance.note: Entirely about vedanā feeling as turning point
